rows.summary: The 2021 FDD introduces state-specific addenda (Maryland, Minnesota, Rhode Island, Virginia) that override or limit core contractual provisions in Item 17 — particularly the mandatory Montreal arbitration clause, general release enforceability, choice-of-law (Quebec), and forum selection. Maryland amendments exempt claims under its franchise law from general releases and permit litigation in Maryland courts with a 3-year statute of limitations. Minnesota addendum voids out-of-state litigation requirements, mandates 90/180-day termination/non-renewal notice, imposes indemnification obligations, and prohibits jury trial waivers. Rhode Island voids forum/law clauses for claims under its Act. These are material contractual modifications limiting franchisor control over dispute resolution and renewal/termination mechanics.
